sentence_reviews.text: Populations of candidate solutions are selected, mutated, recombined, or coordinated without gradient requirements.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA contents: Search in Complex Environments, optimization/search, and learning chapters; no directly inspected genetic/evolutionary algorithm passage.
sentence_reviews.notes: The definition matches standard evolutionary/swarm methods, but the inspected packet source is only a broad AIMA pointer and does not directly support every operator or the no-gradient clause.

sentence_reviews.text: The role of Evolutionary, swarm & population-based learning is to connect Optimization theory, Probability theory to downstream capabilities including AutoML & automated model design, Neural architecture search.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA contents: optimization/search and probability chapters; Elsken et al. NAS survey abstract: automated architecture search, categorized by search space, search strategy, performance estimation.
sentence_reviews.notes: Optimization/probabilistic search and NAS/AutoML connections are defensible across sources, but not directly stated as this full role sentence.

sentence_reviews.text: substrate for AutoML & automated model design
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Elsken et al. 2018 arXiv abstract and subject categories: automated neural architecture search methods; Neural and Evolutionary Computing subject classification.
sentence_reviews.notes: The NAS survey supports evolutionary computing as part of automated architecture search, but the broader AutoML substrate wording is wider than the inspected evidence.

sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Elsken et al. 2018 arXiv page: NAS survey, search-space/search-strategy/performance-estimation framing; cs.NE subject.
sentence_reviews.notes: The source supports evolutionary computing relevance to NAS, although the abstract does not explicitly state every NAS method is evolutionary/population-based.

sentence_reviews.text: The chosen learning signal and interaction pattern may not match the deployment objective or data-generating process. Its direct dependencies include Optimization theory, Probability theory.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA contents: optimization/search and probability chapters; no inspected passage on this limitation for evolutionary learning.
sentence_reviews.notes: Generic limitation text is not adequately supported by inspected evidence.

sentence_reviews.text: Evolutionary, swarm & population-based learning uses Optimization theory as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA contents: Solving Problems by Searching and Search in Complex Environments; optimization background.
sentence_reviews.notes: Population methods are optimization/search methods, but the available inspected AIMA evidence is broad rather than direct.

sentence_reviews.text: Evolutionary, swarm & population-based learning uses Probability theory as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA contents: Quantifying Uncertainty and probabilistic reasoning chapters; no direct evolutionary algorithm passage inspected.
sentence_reviews.notes: Stochastic mutation/selection and swarm updates make probability plausible, but direct support is absent.

sentence_reviews.text: Evolutionary, swarm & population-based learning uses Game theory & decision theory as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA contents: Making Simple Decisions, Making Complex Decisions, Multiagent Decision Making; no direct evolutionary/population connection inspected.
sentence_reviews.notes: Game/decision theory may apply to some population dynamics, but the inspected evidence does not establish it as a common dependency.

edge_reviews.notes: Direction and uses type are plausible; direct evidence from the cited packet source was not inspected beyond broad AIMA contents.
Stochastic population methods support probability as an ingredient, but core strength is not directly supported by the inspected source.
No inspected evidence supports game/decision theory as a common dependency for evolutionary, swarm, and population-based learning.
